purpose: Fixed-position terminal-style layout for the original Portal credits page. It places the lyric box, credits box, and ASCII art picture area on a black monospace canvas. Surfaces a :root --text-color CSS custom property (default #ff0 yellow) so portal/cake.js applyTextColor can override the page color via a hex string. Also defines the .color-cycle keyframe animation (hue-rotate 60s loop, suppressed by prefers-reduced-motion) and the body.color-rainbow > #lyricstext/#creditstext > *:nth-child(6n+k) palette (yellow / amber / red / magenta / cyan / mint) that recolors lyric and credit spans cyclically.
invariants: Dimensions are expressed in em units that match the hard-coded border character counts in portal/cake.js.
The #picture region must stay present for cake.setPicture output.

#picture left-aligns with #credits at 33em and spans 24em vertically so its bottom matches #lyrics bottom (54em). #picturetext uses flexbox to center ASCII art frames on both axes; line-height 1.2em makes 20-line frames exactly fill the 24em box.

:root --text-color is the contract surface for A5 text-color modes; body color resolves through this var, so portal/cake.js applyTextColor() can swap the page color by setting it on document.documentElement.style. The default #ff0 is identical to the original 'yellow' keyword.
.color-cycle and .color-rainbow are mutually exclusive body classes — A5 launcher writes one or the other (never both) based on the user's selected mode.

Rainbow palette is 6 colors (yellow / amber / red / magenta / cyan / mint) repeated via :nth-child(6n+k); rules apply ONLY to direct children of #lyricstext and #creditstext, so the borders (#lyricsleft etc.) stay yellow even in rainbow mode.
gotchas: The layout is not responsive; widening or shrinking content can easily misalign the text-drawn borders.

Hue-rotate animation on body.color-cycle affects EVERYTHING inside body — ASCII art, borders, lyric text, credit text. This is intentional; the whole page hue-cycles in lockstep. If a future element needs to escape the cycle, isolate it via `filter: none` reset or move it outside body.
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) disables the cycle animation; the body stays at hue-rotate(0deg) so colors are still yellow.
